jobs.title: Real Estate - Asset Management, Analyst
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Real Estate – Asset Management, Analyst

Business Unit: Real Estate

Job Title: Analyst

Location: New York, NY

Business Unit Overview:

Blackstone is a global leader in real estate investing. Blackstone’s real estate business was founded in 1991 and has US $315 billion of investor capital under management. Blackstone is the largest owner of commercial real estate globally, owning and operating assets across every major geography and sector, including logistics, data centers, residential, office and hospitality. Our opportunistic funds seek to acquire undermanaged, well-located assets across the world. Blackstone’s Core+ business invests in substantially stabilized real estate assets globally, through both institutional strategies and strategies tailored for income-focused individual investors including Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust, Inc. (BREIT). Blackstone Real Estate also operates one of the leading global real estate debt businesses, providing comprehensive financing solutions across the capital structure and risk spectrum, including management of Blackstone Mortgage Trust (NYSE: BXMT).

Job Description:

Analysts focused on asset management will support post-acquisition transactions including restructurings/recapitalizations, refinancing, dispositions (public IPOs and private market sales), as well as the strategy and execution of value enhancement projects, including lease and management negotiations, capital expenditure execution, and platform company strategy and direction.

Analysts at Blackstone have the opportunity to participate in all aspects of projects and can expect to be staffed on a number of assignments at one time. Typical work includes:

  • Building and maintaining detailed financial models (go-forward return models, next buyer models, DCFs, development pro formas)
  • Building analyses to support investment, financing, capital and sale decisions
  • Real estate and public company valuation analysis
  • Supporting due diligence and transaction execution under senior team direction
  • Assisting in the preparation of materials for deal counterparties, external advisors, and platform company leadership
  • Attending site tours
  • Assisting with legal negotiations and due diligence
  • Assisting in the development of investment budgets, strategic plans, and asset valuations
  • Tracking portfolio performance metrics and preparing recurring reports
  • Excel financial modeling
  • Drafting of memoranda and presentations for internal and external use
  • Market research and data analysis

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors.

The successful candidate should have the following qualifications:

  • 1+ years of relevant experience; internship experience in investment banking, real estate, or finance preferred
  • Strong academic record
  • Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint
  • ·Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • A desire to work in a team environment with dynamic roles
  • Strong understanding of accounting, finance, and economics
  • Eagerness to learn in a fast-paced environment
  • Sincere commitment to professional development
  • Excellent attention to detail
  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Good judgment
  • Real estate coursework or demonstrated interest in real estate investing a plus


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

Expected annual base salary range:

$100,000 - $125,000

Actual base salary within that range will be determined by several components including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills, qualifications and job location. For roles located outside of the US, please disregard the posted salary bands as these roles will follow a separate compensation process based on local market comparables.

Additional compensation and benefits offered in connection with the role consist of comprehensive health benefits, including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, and FSA benefits; paid time off; life insurance; 401(k) plan; and discretionary bonuses. Certain employees may also be eligible for equity and other incentive compensation at Blackstone’s sole discretion.

jobs.description:

We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. 2027 Blackstone Private Equity Associate (San Francisco)

Business Unit Overview:

With $169 billion of assets under management, Blackstone’s corporate private equity business has been a global leader since 1985. We uncover value by identifying great companies and enhancing their performance by providing strategic capital and outstanding management talent. We aim to grow stronger enterprises, create jobs, and enable our portfolio companies to build lasting value for our investors, their employees and all stakeholders.    

Associate Program Overview:

The Associate Program at Blackstone provides a unique opportunity to enhance a comprehensive set of skills that combines the financial acumen of investment banking with the strategic insight of management consulting. Associates engage in the full investment lifecycle, from sourcing and evaluating investments to executing transactions and supporting portfolio companies. With a relatively small team size, associates take on significant responsibilities within deal teams, playing integral roles in the development, structuring, and financing of transactions. They participate in internal meetings, negotiations, and due diligence sessions, gaining hands-on experience and exposure to critical decision-making processes. This immersive environment supports professional growth and provides associates with opportunities to improve essential skills in financial analysis, strategic thinking, and operational insight.

Blackstone Capital Partners (BCP) San Francisco offers Associates a unique opportunity to engage in the acquisition of large-cap and midsized companies across the technology sector.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Conduct thorough research to identify and evaluate potential investment opportunities in various sectors.
  • Perform comprehensive financial analysis, including valuation and modeling, to assess investment prospects.
  • Analyze industry trends and competitive dynamics to inform strategic decision-making.
  • Execute detailed business diligence to evaluate target companies, including their operations, financial health, and growth potential.
  • Prepare comprehensive due diligence packs that incorporate relevant analytics, market research, and background information.
  • Conduct interviews with industry experts and management teams to gather insights and validate investment assumptions.
  • Assist in the execution and financing of transactions, ensuring effective coordination among stakeholders.
  • Build detailed operating models for prospective investments to project financial outcomes and assess value creation strategies.
  • Draft clear and concise investment memoranda for internal and external stakeholders.
  • Prepare written summaries and presentation materials to effectively communicate findings and investment theses to the investment committee.
  • Support portfolio companies by analyzing performance metrics, identifying opportunities for operational improvements, develop and implement value creation plans for portfolio investments.
  • Provide overall support to the investment team, contributing to various projects and initiatives as needed.
  • Embrace an apprenticeship-based model, actively learning from and sharing knowledge with team members to foster a collaborative environment.

Qualifications:

  • Requires a BA or BSc degree.
  • 2+ years of experience in investment banking or private equity.
  • Strong understanding of accounting and finance.
  • Proficient in financial modeling and analysis.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills; effectively present complex information clearly.
  • Effective interpersonal skills and works collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.
  • Manage multiple competing priorities and demonstrate effective time management skills.
  • High level of motivation, attention to detail, and a strong desire to learn and grow.

San Francisco Applicants: Blackstone will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records, consistent with the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.  


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

$140,000 - $140,000

Actual base salary within that range will be determined by several components including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills, qualifications and job location. For roles located outside of the US, please disregard the posted salary bands as these roles will follow a separate compensation process based on local market comparables.

jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Job Description: The Blackstone Life Sciences (BXLS) Finance team is responsible for the investment operations, fund administration, and financial planning and reporting needs of the Life Sciences group based in Cambridge, MA. The BXLS Finance team leads the execution and management of investment transactions, monitors investment portfolios, and ensures compliance with all regulatory requirements. The BXLS Finance team works closely with the investment team and both internal and external stakeholders to produce timely and accurate investment operations and financial reporting. The BXLS Finance team is also responsible for financial planning and analysis (FP&A), which entails revenue and expense forecasting, sensitivity analysis, fund modeling, and preparation of earnings-related materials.

BXLS is searching for candidates with specific interests in:

  • Investment Fund Portfolio Management
  • Technology and Process Improvement

Key responsibilities include but are not limited to:

  • Process ownership of the execution, settlement, and tracking of all investment transactions at the funds and portfolio investment levels
  • Preparing investment allocations and funds flows
  • Interpreting complex fund documents and legal agreements
  • Building automated reporting and reducing manual processes
  • Creating and presenting internal reporting materials to Blackstone Senior Leadership
  • Preparing and reviewing materials for investors, including memos, financial forecasts, meeting materials, and presentations
  • Working closely with the investment team on various ad hoc requests and projects
  • Collaborating with teams across the firm and globe, including Accounting, Valuations, Tax, Legal & Compliance, Investor Relations, Audit, and offshore teams

Qualifications:

The successful candidate must have:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or other Business-related field
  • 1+ years of work experience in the Asset Management or Financial Services industry
  • Understanding of investment instruments and key financial metrics
  • Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills with keen attention to detail
  • Proven capability to work in a dynamic environment and handle multiple projects by prioritizing tasks and meeting deadlines
  • Possess a strong work ethic and professional integrity, a team player
  • Excellent time management and organizational skills
  • A positive attitude and strong desire to work in an entrepreneurial environment
  • Effective communication skills, both written and verbal with a talent for presenting complex information in a clear and concise manner

jobs.title: Private Capital Group, VP
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .
 

Private Capital Group London (VP)

Blackstone is seeking an experienced business development professional to proactively develop new clients and expand relationships with sophisticated family offices in UK. This individual will build a 360-degree dialogue with clients and deliver customized access to Blackstone’s integrated multi-asset platform. The goal is to establish and deepen relationships with UK clients, grow assets under management, and effectively communicate the suite of offerings to prospective clients with a credible, advanced depth of investment and portfolio knowledge.

  • Identify and cultivate high quality relationships with family offices; this includes developing goals, objectives, strategies, and tactics to create meaningful relationships with family offices, RIAs and endowments and foundations, resulting in a consistent awareness of Blackstone’s strategies and growth in assets under management
  • Understand and effectively communicate complex alternative investment capabilities to existing and prospective clients in a straightforward fashion, in both verbal and written form.
  • Explain how Blackstone’s solutions compare to competitors and how these varied products/solutions can help clients fill gaps in their portfolios and reach their investment objectives
  • Maintain a high level of prospecting activity, including continuous in-person touchpoints with clients to build rapport, trust, and connectivity
  • Collaborate with the firm’s product and investment teams to facilitate new business onboarding and client cross-selling activities
  • Regularly obtain feedback from families on their perspective of Blackstone’s offerings, to inform product positioning, future product strategy and business approach
  • Partner closely with Blackstone’s marketing team to develop collateral material and communications focused on education and product positioning
  • Support and encourage a team-oriented model with effective communication, transparency and trust
  • Participate strategically in industry conferences as appropriate
     

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors.
 

The successful candidate must have:

  • 6-10 years of directly related sales experience for alternative investment strategies
  • Demonstrated track record of success in identifying and building long-term professional and raising assets; experience with family offices, RIAs and endowments and foundations
  • Possess excellent knowledge of alternative investment strategies (including private equity, real estate, hedge funds, and credit), capital markets, portfolio management and asset allocation
  • Comfort engaging with clients, including family office Chief Investment Officers, in solutions-oriented discussions; effectively position the firm’s varied products and solutions to help clients reach their investment objectives
  • Desire to work both independently and collaboratively in a dynamic environment, and collaborate across business units
  • Deep understanding of trends and buying behavior for institutional quality investors, as it relates to their use of alternative investments within existing asset allocation
  • Strong communication and presentation skills, coupled with the ability to effectively position Blackstone and its capabilities to investors
  • Strong organizational skills; ability to prioritize targets and handle a busy calendar of clients meetings
  • Client-oriented; leveraging effective relationship management and interpersonal skills, with a considerable understanding of how to achieve targeted levels of client confidence
  • Self-starter, entrepreneurial and goal oriented, with a high level of accountability and drive
  • Some travel required
  • Bachelor’s Degree; advanced Degree and/or CFA preferred

jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .
 

Business Unit:

Blackstone Private Wealth, Tokyo

Business Unit Overview:

Blackstone Private Wealth (BXPW) was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms as well as family offices, Blackstone’s Private Wealth Solutions team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capability to these firms and their clients and provides ongoing product and advisor support, as well as education and training around alternatives.

Job Title: Product Strategy, Analyst/Associate

Global Product Strategy:

Global Product Strategy is focused on designing flagship, category-killing products that deliver Blackstone’s investment capabilities, redefining the accessibility of alternatives for private wealth clients. As a member of the team, the individual will be responsible for driving the design and end-to-end development of new funds. The ideal candidate will be creative and passionate about researching and creating innovative product structures and solutions to unlock new markets and distribution channels.

The team provides domain knowledge and expertise on investments, performance, market dynamics and competitors. Global Product Strategy has key internal and external stakeholders, including Blackstone investment teams, Blackstone Private Wealth sales professionals, banking teams, financial advisors, and clients.

Job Description:

The Japan Product Strategist will focus on product development, supporting Blackstone Private Wealth's initiatives mainly in Japan.

Primary job responsibilities will include:

  • Drive and supporting the development of Blackstone Private Wealth's business in Japan by creating products that are relevant to the local client base.

  • Develop domestic and international structures/solutions mindful of appropriate regulatory frameworks and constraints.

  • Play a significant role interfacing with members of the firm’s global Blackstone Private Wealth group's across all Blackstone investment funds, legal/compliance department, finance and other groups within the firm.

  • Conduct research on product structures, regulatory developments and competitor fund launches to help identify scale opportunities.

  • Work with internal Blackstone teams on the full scope of product development, including the buildout of operations, technology, marketing and finance.

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. 

The successful candidate must have:

  • Minimum 3 years’ relevant work experience

  • Deep knowledge of fund structuring and regulations

  • Relevant product development experience in asset management industry

  • Knowledge of alternative funds and strategies

  • A high level of attention to detail

  • Fluent in Japanese with business-level proficiency in English

  • Great presentation skills

  • A desire to work in a team environment, often against competing deadlines

  • Experience in project management and if the experience is across global/NY-based teams

  • Strong Excel and PowerPoint skills

Business Unit Overview:

Blackstone Private Wealth was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms as well as family offices, Blackstone’s Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capability to these firms and their clients and provides ongoing product and advisor support, as well as education and training around alternatives.

Job Title:

Sales & Promotion, Analyst/Associate

Job Description:

The role will be involved in building relationships and maintaining contacts with distributors and asset management companies (sub-advisory partners), with the goal of accelerating the adoption of Blackstone investment solutions and growing the private wealth business in Japan.

The role will be responsible for understanding and articulating Blackstone’s sophisticated products (fund objectives, terms, exposures, characteristics, positioning, performance, etc.), providing promotion activities by conducting teach-in sessions and end-client seminars for distributor (at branches or via online) to support persistent growth of clients’ assets through the product lifecycle and maintaining client services by responding to inbound inquiries and working with professionals across Blackstone (including investments, legal/compliance department, finance, and other groups within the firm) to resolve clients’ issues.

  • Develop strong understanding of distribution partners’ operational platforms and processes and become resident Blackstone expert for advisors.

  • Assist Financial Advisors with problem resolution of account opening, maintenance items and other inquiries related to client onboarding, servicing and client retention (deadlines, platform questions, redemptions, capital calls, distributions).

jobs.title: Blackstone Private Wealth - Product Specialist (BXHF), Principal
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Blackstone Private Wealth was established to meet the growing demand for Blackstone investment solutions from high-net-worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s leading private banks, wealth management platforms, and family offices, the Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative investment capabilities. The platform provides ongoing product support, advisor engagement, and education across alternative asset classes. Leveraging Blackstone’s global scale across Private Equity, Secondaries, Real Estate, Credit, Tactical Opportunities, and Hedge Fund Solutions, the Private Wealth business structures both single-asset-class and multi-asset solutions aligned with clients’ asset allocation and portfolio construction objectives. Blackstone manages approximately $300 billion of capital from Private Wealth clients and operates globally with offices in New York, London, Zurich, Paris, Singapore, Hong Kong, and Tokyo, serving many of the world’s largest wealth management institutions, independent advisors, and family offices.

Role Overview

Product Specialists within Blackstone Private Wealth play a critical role in delivering deep investment expertise to internal and external stakeholders. They support the full product lifecycle, from fundraising and client onboarding through portfolio construction, ongoing investment oversight, and return-of-capital. Product Specialists serve as trusted partners, providing insight on investment strategy, portfolio positioning, performance drivers, market dynamics, and competitive positioning. This Principal role will focus on Blackstone’s Hedge Fund Solutions platform, and will place an emphasis on delivering multi-strategy and absolute return solutions for private wealth clients. The role includes both direct product coverage and leadership oversight of professionals supporting Hedge Fund Solutions across the Private Wealth platform.

Key Responsibilities

  • Partner closely with Hedge Fund Solutions investment teams and cross-functional internal stakeholders to develop and execute comprehensive sales and distribution strategies across client segments, including product positioning, messaging, content development, and competitive differentiation
  • Act as a senior subject-matter expert, delivering timely, high-conviction insights on portfolio construction, performance attribution, risk management, liquidity, and market developments to sales teams, financial advisors, end-clients, portfolio managers, and other key stakeholders
  • Develop, curate, and maintain high-impact, institutionally rigorous content tailored to Private Wealth channels, including monthly market and portfolio commentary, quarterly product reviews, thematic insights, strategy updates, and responses to bespoke client and due diligence requests
  • Collaborate across Private Wealth functions, including Sales, Marketing, Product Strategy, Investor Services, Legal, Compliance, and Business Development, to support product launches, platform enhancements, and strategic growth initiatives
  • Lead and mentor a team of Product Specialists, fostering a culture of intellectual rigor, accountability, and client-centric execution
  • Conduct and maintain ongoing research into the hedge fund and semi-liquid product landscape, including strategy evolution, peer performance, structural considerations, and regulatory developments, to ensure best-in-class product positioning
     

Qualifications

In addition to a minimum of 12+ years of experience in asset management, investment banking, consulting, hedge funds, or wealth management, the successful candidate will demonstrate:

  • Deep familiarity with hedge fund solutions, multi-strategy portfolios, semi-liquid products, and portfolio construction within a private wealth or institutional context
  • FINRA Series 7 & (63/65 or 66) required.
  • Strong executive presence with credibility and thoughtful engagement with senior investment professionals, advisors, and sophisticated clients
  • High emotional intelligence and judgment, paired with strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, can distill complex investment concepts into clear, compelling narratives
  • Strong organizational skills and effectiveness in managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced, client-focused setting
  • Solid understanding of global financial markets, securities, investment strategies, and the relevant regulatory framework
  • Willingness to travel for in-person client and advisor engagement
  • MBA and/or CFA preferred, but not required


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

Expected annual base salary range:

$200,000 - $250,000

Actual base salary within that range will be determined by several components including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills, qualifications and job location. For roles located outside of the US, please disregard the posted salary bands as these roles will follow a separate compensation process based on local market comparables.

Additional compensation and benefits offered in connection with the role consist of comprehensive health benefits, including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, and FSA benefits; paid time off; life insurance; 401(k) plan; and discretionary bonuses. jobs.title: Associate - Strategic Advisory (India Coverage)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Advisory group is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions of the past twenty years.  Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including, technology media telecommunications, natural resources, power & utilities, industrials, healthcare, and real estate.

Associates within Advisory would be responsible for the following:

  • Evaluating, structuring, and recommending financial and strategic alternatives, including the analysis of mergers, acquisitions, and other strategic combinations, asset sales, and divestures.

  • Leading the day-to-day execution of transactions, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiating of contracts and other agreements; work with senior management and coordinate analyst materials.

  • Analysing financial data and developments in financial markets important to the business of PJT Partners.

  • Conducting research of targeted industries for investment purposes.

  • Performing valuation analysis of prospective investment opportunities under consideration, including the assessment of financial risks.

  • Comparing a company’s financial data with other companies of the same industry, size, and location to the forecast of industry trends.

  • Designing and developing quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies.

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and review by clients.

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

  • Mentoring and training of juniors

The Associate will be a critical member of PJT’s expanding India Coverage team with direct involvement in both deal execution and business development, working on lean deal teams with direct exposure to the most senior practitioners. The role will span across a number of sectors. Given the lean nature of our teams all our bankers are flexible and should be prepared to work in sectors/countries outside their main area of focus.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in finance, law, accountancy or business management from a Government recognised university or institution, or from an overseas university of institution

  • Minimum of 3 years’ directly comparable experience, including at least 2 years within investment banking at an India based merchant bank and with a strong focus on M&A

  • Certificate for NISM Series-IX: Merchant Banking Certification Examination

  • A strong background in finance/accounting is required

  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills

  • Excellent communication skills

  • Resume must be in PDF format  

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance.

Applicant Privacy Notice:

View our Applicant Privacy Noticehttps://info.pjtpartners.com/PJT_Global_Applicant_Privacy_Noticehere. If you are a California resident, please refer to our California Applicant Privacy Noticehttps://info.pjtpartners.com/California_Applicant_Privacy_Noticefor further information.

*In order to be considered, please ensure your resume/CV is submitted in PDF format.

jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Strategic Advisory Group is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions of the past twenty years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including, technology media telecommunications, energy, power & utilities, industrials, healthcare, real estate gaming and leisure, and consumer retail.

The Strategic Capital Markets Advisory team is an extremely active and fast-growing group within PJT. The team is based across the New York and London offices with frequent collaboration across regions. The London team has been active on some of the most high-profile transactions in the market over the recent past, including for Aggreko, ASDA, ASOS, Arrow Global, Alstom, DIA, EG Group, EGYM, EIP, Job&Talent, and Porsche SE (amongst others).

As an Associate in the Strategic Capital Markets Advisory team, you will sit within the Advisory Group but also work frequently with the Restructuring and Special Situations Group who advise companies, financial sponsors and creditor groups in financially distressed situations.

  • You will work directly with financial sponsor and corporate clients across all industries in evaluating, structuring, recommending and executing financing alternatives across the capital structure

  • You will have the opportunity to advise clients on a broad range of transactions including (i) syndicated acquisition financings and refinancings, (ii) private credit lending, (iii) par and stressed refinancings, (iv) liability management, including A&Es and Exchange Offers, (v) growth and venture capital, and (vi) structured and infrastructure financings

  • The team sits at the fulcrum of PJT’s M&A and Restructuring franchises, thereby offering unparalleled exposure to clients and senior bankers, as well as exceptional deal-flow

  • Day-to-day responsibilities include analysing capital structures and cashflow models, credit documents and indentures; facilitating due diligence and discussions with companies, legal counsel and advisors; ratings analyses; and direct interaction with clients, banks and funds on capital raising transactions

  • Associates will be expected to manage analysts staffed on their transactions and on internal and external business development projects.

Qualifications

To be considered for the position, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in a top ranked university

  • 3-4 years of experience in leveraged finance, debt capital markets advisory, restructuring or direct lending at an investment bank or credit fund

  • ACA or CFA qualification preferred but not mandatory

  • Resume must include graduation month/year and GPA, if applicable

  • Resume must be in PDF format  

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance.

Applicant Privacy Notice:

View our Applicant Privacy Noticehttps://info.pjtpartners.com/PJT_Global_Applicant_Privacy_Noticehere. If you are a California resident, please refer to our California Applicant Privacy Noticehttps://info.pjtpartners.com/California_Applicant_Privacy_Noticefor further information.

*In order to be considered, please ensure your resume/CV is submitted in PDF format.

The Strategic Advisory group is one of the most experienced teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions in recent years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including financial institutions, technology, media & telecommunications, natural resources, power & utilities, industrials healthcare, and real estate.

The Strategic Capital Markets Advisory team is an extremely active and fast-growing group within PJT. The team is based across the New York and London offices with frequent collaboration across regions. The team has >100 professionals globally focused on equity, private capital, debt and structured products. The global Equity Capital Markets team has been active on some of the most high-profile transactions in the market for both sponsors and corporates, including for Boeing, The NFL, General Electric, Visa, Alstom, Worldline, Porsche SE, EGYM, Sodexo, Solvay, EG Group, ASDA and ASOS.

  • Associates within the Capital Markets Advisory Group work directly with public and private companies across all stages of growth and industries in evaluating, structuring and recommending financial alternatives across equity and equity linked products

  • Associates typically work on a wide variety of equity transactions including IPOs, spin-offs, rights issues, M&A financings, equity linked, derivatives, minority investments and private placements

  • PJT’s structure offers professionals the unique opportunity to gain client exposure and work closely with senior members of the Strategic Advisory team, many of whom were previously group heads at leading investment banking firms

  • Day-to-day responsibilities include: running ECM related processes, analysing capital structures and defining capital needs, evaluating different equity capital raise options, facilitating due diligence and discussions with investors, companies, legal counsel and advisors

  • Associates receive broad-based exposure to the full spectrum of complex issues that arise in capital markets transactions, including capital raising through both the public and private markets, mergers and acquisitions, growth financing, asset-level financing and others

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s Degree

  • c.3-5 years of experience in equity capital markets, equity linked, equity derivatives or sector investment banking team at a leading investment bank

  • Resume must include graduation month/year and GPA

  • In addition to strong analytical and quantitative skills, the successful candidate should have excellent communication skills, a demonstrated ability to write effectively, interests outside of work and a desire to work in a team environment 

  • A strong background in finance, accounting and legal documentation is required as you will be responsible for structuring and executing financing transactions, including equity, equity-linked, and private capital solutions

  • Resume must be in PDF format

jobs.title: Associate - PJT Park Hill (Private Equity - Distribution)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

Responsibilities

Our PJT Park Hill Private Equity team is seeking an Associate to join the San Francisco office. The successful candidate will be tasked with supporting distribution efforts across the West Coast. They will be responsible for maintaining existing LP relationships as well as developing new relationships.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Cultivate strong LP and GP relationships; understand how to navigate these relationships to maximize commercial impact and influence key stakeholders;
  • Maintain a deep understanding of both investors and clients, key selling points and challenges around mandates and how best to address with investors;
  • Anticipate investor base needs; use knowledge to direct both sales and sourcing efforts;
  • Provide advice to LP clients on market trends and/or strategies that go beyond PJT Park Hill mandates;
  • “Quarterback” the lifecycle of the sale. Play an integral role throughout the negotiation, structuring, distributing and closing phases of complex transactions;
  • Work effectively with existing project management teams across the firm to ensure effective communication and dialogue resulting in constantly improving distribution results.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor degree required with Accounting, Finance, or Economics major preferred;

  • 5 -10 years of fundraising experience, particularly with private equity;
  • Strong investor relationships and substantial experience in managing capital raises;
  • Ability to drive multiple sales processes simultaneously;
  • Demonstrable track record of building client relationships;
  • Strong knowledge of the markets / competitive landscape;
  • Ability to work in a fast paced, dynamic environment and to handle multiple projects at a time;
  • A self-starter, but with the ability to work effectively as part of a global team;
  • Confident and self-aware with excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal;
  • Strong work ethic; genuine passion for excellence; results-oriented;
  • Personal and professional integrity of the highest order;
  • Entrepreneurial outlook;
  • Self-starter who takes initiative and is collaborative both internally and externally.

Expected annualized base salary of $225,000 to $250,000.

This range represents the low and high ends of the expected annualized base salary for this position. The specific base salary received by any employee will be informed by a number of factors, including but not limited to, role level and scope, location, candidate’s relevant qualifications, skills and experience.

Base salary is one component of PJT Partners’ compensation structure. In addition to base salary, PJT Partners’ compensation structure may include additional rewards, incentives and benefits, including but not limited to, a discretionary bonus component.

jobs.title: Associate - Strategic Advisory (Telecom and Digital Infrastructure)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. The Strategic Advisory group is one of the most experienced teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions in recent years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including financial institutions, technology, media & telecommunications, natural resources, power & utilities, industrials healthcare, and real estate.

PJT Partners’ Strategic Advisory Telecom and Digital Infrastructure team in London, is seeking an Associate who would be responsible for the following:

  • Evaluating, structuring, and recommending financial and strategic alternatives, including the analysis of mergers, acquisitions, and other strategic combinations, asset sales, and divestures.

  • Leading the day-to-day execution of transactions, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiating of contracts and other agreements; work with senior management and coordinate analyst materials.

  • Analysing financial data and developments in financial markets important to the business of PJT Partners.

  • Conducting research of targeted industries for investment purposes.

  • Performing valuation analysis of prospective investment opportunities under consideration, including the assessment of financial risks.

  • Comparing a company’s financial data with other companies of the same industry, size, and location to the forecast of industry trends.

  • Designing and developing quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies.

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and review by clients.

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

  • Mentoring and training of juniors

The Associate will be a critical member of PJT’s TMT team with direct involvement in both deal execution and business development, working on lean deal teams with direct exposure to the most senior practitioners. The role will span across a number of sectors. Given the lean nature of our teams all our bankers are flexible and should be prepared to work in sectors/countries outside their main area of focus.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s Degree

  • Minimum 3 years of directly comparable (Investment Banking) experience with particular focus on M&A

  •  Full breadth of financing (equity, equity linked and debt) and strategic advisory experience

  • Knowledge of and experience in the TMT industry, specifically telecommunications, technology and digital infrastructure, and related transactions preferred

  • Experience with financial modelling including developing detailed operating and valuation models for complex transactions

  • Professional maturity and experience to interact directly with clients and drive origination and execution

  • Ability to manage several projects at once and work effectively as an individual and as part of a team

  • Fluency in English is required and in another European language preferred

  • Resume must be in PDF format  

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. jobs.title: Deal Assistant - Park Hill (Private Equity - Infrastructure)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. Responsibilities

This is a strong opportunity for someone who can work independently and as part of a team. The Deal Assistant plays a key role in supporting the deal-making process. The ideal candidate will have a variety of responsibilities, which will include:

  • Acting as gatekeeper to professionals and maintaining relationships with/act as resource for clients, investors, prospective clients and internal employees;
  • Managing calendars, schedule appointments, and confirm meetings with investors and clients;
  • Arranging travel for professionals, clients and investors, including flights, cars, hotels, and visas;
  • Tracking, preparing, and processing expense reconciliations, out-of-pocket reports, and invoices;
  • Scheduling conference rooms for internal and external meetings; coordinate supporting technology and refreshments as needed;
  • Greeting guests; work with reception to register guests in visitor system;
  • Covering multiple phone lines, and communicating with clients as requested;
  • Attending team meetings, reviewing agenda with professionals beforehand, taking and distributing meeting notes;
  • Sending and drafting email correspondence on behalf of professionals;
  • Handling confidential documents and correspondence as requested;
  • Tracking and maintaining entry of data and data integrity in a systematic manner through databases including Outlook, Deal Cloud, and Mailings;
  • Managing mail merges; create working group lists;
  • Managing fundraise schedule and keep deal calendar updated;
  • Working with clients on deal books and road shows; managing client budgets for road shows and events;
  • Coordinating events and road shows (prepare budgets, find and book venues, negotiate vendor contracts, create invitations and order giveaways) in conjunction with the events team.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Strong Microsoft Office skills (emphasis on Outlook)
  • High level of attention to detail
  • Excellent writing and communication skills
  • Ability to interact professionally with internal and external stakeholders, including executives, clients and peers
  • Ability to multi-task and independently manage competing priorities
  • Exceptional organizational skills to effectively manage multiple projects 
  • Ability to exercise initiative; be proactive in anticipating professionals’ and clients’ needs
  • Experience using Deal Cloud is preferred but not required
  • Experience using Workday is preferred but not required
  • Strong work ethic who thrives in a collaborative and team-oriented culture.

Expected annualized base salary of $85,000 - $100,000.

This range represents the low and high ends of the expected annualized base salary for this position. The specific base salary received by any employee will be informed by a number of factors, including but not limited to, role level and scope, location, candidate’s relevant qualifications, skills and experience.

jobs.title: Vice President - Restructuring and Special Situations Group
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Restructuring and Special Situations Group advises large companies, governments and creditor groups in financially distressed situations. The group has been involved in most of the largest and most high-profile reorganizations that have occurred in the last decade. In a restructuring transaction, a wide range of strategic advisory services are provided, including advice on recapitalizations, reorganizations, corporate valuations, exchange offers, distressed mergers and acquisitions and raising debt and equity capital.

VPs in the Restructuring and Special Situations Group have the opportunity to develop a diverse and broad corporate finance skill set. Financial restructuring transactions are complex and involve multi-party negotiations with a number of key stakeholders including, among others, the board of directors, management, secured and unsecured lenders and equity holders.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Helping to lead the origination and execution of restructuring transactions across our platform;
  • Frequent senior-level interaction with clients and investors;
  • Developing detailed business plans;
  • Developing strategies to implement various restructuring transactions;
  • Evaluating financial returns to current and potential investors;
  • Attending key client negotiating and strategy sessions;
  • Managing complex and often time-sensitive restructuring transactions from start to finish;
  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • At least 6 years of work experience in corporate concentrated in restructuring, leveraged & acquisition finance or credit
  • Fluency in English is required
  • Bachelor’s or Masters degree
  • Strong qualitative and analytical skills
  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to run client meetings and coordinate deal execution
  • Strong written and presentation skills
  • A desire to work in a team environment, often under tight deadlines
  • Strong knowledge of accounting and finance
  • Ability to mentor and train more junior bankers
  • Personal and professional integrity

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

jobs.title: Analyst - PJT Park Hill (Private Capital Solutions)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

PJT Park Hill (“Park Hill”) is our industry-leading advisor and intermediary business focused on alternative investment vehicles in the private equity, real estate, hedge fund and secondary advisory asset classes.

The Private Capital Solutions group, our secondary advisory practice, is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions since the team’s founding in 2008. The practice leverages the firm’s global relationships to deliver superior results across the secondaries universe. Our highly specialized team focuses on delivering independent advisory and execution services to financial sponsors and institutional investors across a broad spectrum of strategic transactions including:

  • Financial Sponsor Solutions: Fund recapitalizations, continuation vehicles, fund restructurings, tender offers, preferred equity / other debt oriented solutions, management team spinouts and portfolio asset sales

  • Structured Liquidity Solutions: Securitizations, refinancings of illiquid financial holdings and joint ventures 

  • Institutional Investor Solutions: Divesture of private fund interests, valuation of private fund interests, transaction structuring and execution support.

Analysts within the Private Capital Solutions - Secondaries group work directly with clients in evaluating, structuring and recommending financial and strategic alternatives. The remit of the position spans the private capital spectrum with an emphasis on private equity secondary transactions. The role is industry and asset class agnostic, and the Analyst will be a generalist across transactions.

Analysts are key deal team members who are staffed directly on client teams and are expected to be involved in all aspects of a transaction, including due diligence, valuation analysis, client and process management, and investor engagement.

The comparatively small size of this group and the frequent cross-collaboration across PJT offers professionals the unique opportunity to gain client exposure and work closely with senior members across the PJT platform.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • At least 12-24 months of Investment Banking M&A, private equity or secondary advisory experience strongly preferred

  • Outstanding quantitative and analytical skills

  • Strong verbal and written communication skills

  • Proven ability to work collaboratively and effectively within a team environment

  • Exceptional organization and time management skills

  • Strong work ethic and ability to work under pressure

  • Excellent written and spoken English

  • Bachelor’s degree

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

The Strategic Advisory group is one of the most experienced teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions in recent years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including financial institutions, technology, media & telecommunications, natural resources, power & utilities, industrials healthcare, and real estate.

PJT Partners’ Strategic Advisory Industrials team in London, is seeking an Associate who would be responsible for the following:

  • Evaluating, structuring, and recommending financial and strategic alternatives, including the analysis of mergers, acquisitions, and other strategic combinations, asset sales, and divestures.

  • Leading the day-to-day execution of transactions, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiating of contracts and other agreements; work with senior management and coordinate analyst materials.

  • Analysing financial data and developments in financial markets important to the business of PJT Partners.

  • Conducting research of targeted industries for investment purposes.

  • Performing valuation analysis of prospective investment opportunities under consideration, including the assessment of financial risks.

  • Comparing a company’s financial data with other companies of the same industry, size, and location to the forecast of industry trends.

  • Designing and developing quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies.

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and review by clients.

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

  • Mentoring and training of juniors

The team is well established at the Partner and Managing Director levels and is looking for a candidate with relevant industrials experience, a strong interest in working across large and small companies and the personality to thrive as a key part of the industrials team.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s Degree

  • Minimum 3 years of directly comparable (Investment Banking) experience with particular focus on M&A

  •  Full breadth of financing (equity, equity linked and debt) and strategic advisory experience

  • Knowledge of and experience in the Industrials industry,  and related transactions preferred

  • Experience with financial modelling including developing detailed operating and valuation models for complex transactions

  • Professional maturity and experience to interact directly with clients and drive origination and execution

  • Ability to manage several projects at once and work effectively as an individual and as part of a team

  • Fluency in English is required and in another European language preferred

PJT Park Hill’s Alternative Credit and Hedge Fund Group offers analysts the chance to engage directly in advising “best in class” private credit and hedge funds on all aspects of the capital raise – including advisory work, market positioning and ultimately raising capital from the top institutional investors across the globe.

The private placement process uniquely develops both the quantitative and qualitative skills required at more advanced levels across the financial spectrum. Candidates must possess an interest in the alternative asset industry - specifically private credit and hedge funds.

Responsibilities include:

  • Playing an important role in all aspects of the origination effort;
  • Sourcing and performing intensive due diligence on a variety of private credit and hedge fund strategies;

jobs.title: Analyst - PJT Park Hill (Direct Placement)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

PJT Park Hill, our leading global alternative asset advisory and fundraising business, provides private capital advisory and fundraising services for a diverse range of investment strategies. Moreover, PJT Park Hill is the only group among its peers with top-tier dedicated private equity, alternative credit/hedge fund, real estate and secondary advisory groups.

PJT Park Hill’s Direct Placement group offers Analysts the chance to engage directly with top caliber private equity firms by providing a broad range of advisory services, including vehicle formation and capital raising, as well as customized capital solutions for direct, co-investment, and other financing opportunities. The private placement process uniquely develops both the quantitative and qualitative skills required at more advanced levels across financial services.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Assist the deal team in all aspects of execution;
  • Work directly with clients on drafting investment memoranda and marketing materials;
  • Prepare for and coordinate all due diligence meetings and related materials;
  • Help coordinate roadshow scheduling and logistics;
  • Assist with various research projects;
  • Support the sourcing, diligence, and origination of new mandates;
  • Manage and maintain external PJT Park Hill Direct Placement materials, including marketing and pitch materials;
  • Update and maintain deal pipeline offering booklets and executive presentations for PJT Partners.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or Economics preferred, but not required;
  • Knowledge of and interest in the alternative asset industry, specifically private equity;
  • Minimum of 1–2 years of prior finance experience, ideally in private placements, investment banking, alternative asset investor relations, or similar fields;
  • Strong analytical skills, knowledge of financial modeling and leveraged buyout analysis;
  • Detail oriented;
  • Ability to work in a fast paced, dynamic environment and to handle multiple projects at once;
  • A self-starter with the ability to work effectively as part of a team;
  • Confident and self-aware with excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal;
  • Strong work ethic; genuine passion for excellence; results-oriented;
  • High personal and professional integrity;
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.

Expected annualized base salary of $120,000 to $130,000.

This range represents the low and high ends of the expected annualized base salary for this position. The specific base salary received by any employee will be informed by a number of factors, including but not limited to, role level and scope, location, candidate’s relevant qualifications, skills and experience.

Base salary is one component of PJT Partners’ compensation structure. In addition to base salary, PJT Partners’ compensation structure may include additional rewards, incentives and benefits, including but not limited to, a discretionary bonus component.

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance.

jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Strategic Advisory group is one of the most experienced teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions in recent years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including financial institutions, technology, media & telecommunications, natural resources, power & utilities, industrials healthcare, and real estate.

PJT Partners’ Strategic Advisory team in Milan is seeking for an Analyst with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to perform both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm.

Analyst within Strategic Advisory would be responsible for the following: 

  • Act as a key contributor to the execution of transactions, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiation of contracts and other agreements; work in coordination with senior members of the deal team.

  • Perform financial and valuation analysis of perspective investment opportunities under consideration, building and holding responsibility of complex financial models.

  • Contribute to the drafting of presentations and written recommendations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and review by clients.

  • Preparation of memoranda for internal and external use.

  • Conduct financial and other business-related research of targeted companies and key sector trends.

  • Coordinate the delivery of complex and often time-sensitive processes with multiple stakeholders. 

  • Active involvement in client-facing situations, participating in both internal and external client meetings.

  • Assistance in the execution of transactions.

The Analyst will be a critical member of PJT’s expanding Italy Coverage team with direct involvement in both deal execution and business development, working on lean deal teams with direct exposure to PJT’s clients and most senior practitioners. The role will span across a number of sectors. Given the lean nature of our teams all our bankers are flexible and should be prepared to work in sectors/countries outside their main area of focus.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Top class Master’s Degree from a leading university, with preference for a background in Economics, Finance, Accounting or adjacent areas

  • Minimum 1 year of relevant Investment Banking experience with particular focus on M&A and Corporate Finance

  • Fluency in English and Italian is required

  • Demonstrated knowledge of key finance and accounting concepts is required

  • Strong analytical and modelling skills

  • Excellent oral and written communication skills

  • Strong work ethic and ability to work effectively in lean deal teams

  • Resume must be in PDF format  

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law.
